title: Tetraria triangularis

taxonomy: Tetraria triangularis is a species of sedge, which is classified within the family Cyperaceae. This family encompasses a large number of sedges, including several other genera. The Cyperaceae family is part of the order Poales, which contains various monocot families. The order Poales is a part of the clade commelinids, which also includes other plant families. The clade commelinids is a subgroup of the monocot clade, known as the commelinid clade.

intro: Tetraria triangularis, also known as Triangle Club-rush or Triangle Sawgrass, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tetraria triangularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are utilized in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
